gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A definition's **declared** effect set (§4.5 I3) — the set it names on its signature."] pub type EffectSet = BTreeSet < EffectKind > ;
gaps.reason: `type EffectSet = ...` alias — Mycelium's `type_item` always introduces a new nominal sum type via `'=' constructor ('|' constructor)*`; a bare alias to an existing type would fabricate a sum type where none exists semantically

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A performed-but-undeclared effect (I3) — an explicit checker error, never silent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A definition may not perform an effect absent from its declared set.  This is a *checker*"] # [doc = " concern (tooling layer), not a runtime one — it lives here rather than in the shared"] # [doc = " `mycelium-interp` budget primitive (KC-3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct UndeclaredEffect { # [doc = " The effect that was performed but not declared."] pub effect : EffectKind , }
gaps.reason: struct `UndeclaredEffect` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet:
impl std :: fmt :: Display for UndeclaredEffect { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut std :: fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> std :: fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "undeclared effect {}: a definition may not perform an effect absent from its \
             declared signature set (RFC-0014 §4.5 I3 — no unknown side effects); declare it" , self . effect) } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)
item-position macro invocation — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **compositional no-undeclared-effect check** (I3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every effect a definition *performs* (its own plus its callees', composed up the call graph)"] # [doc = " must be in its *declared* set.  Returns the first undeclared effect found, if any."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This *checks* that declared effects compose — it never *infers* one.  An effect can never"] # [doc = " become implicit; the discipline is: **manual-declare + compositional-check** (§8 resolved)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`UndeclaredEffect`] for the first effect in `performed` that is absent from"] # [doc = " `declared`."] pub fn check_effects (declared : & EffectSet , performed : & EffectSet) -> Result < () , UndeclaredEffect > { for e in performed { if ! declared . contains (e) { return Err (UndeclaredEffect { effect : e . clone () }) ; } } Ok (()) }
